st_augustine.name: ST. AUGUSTINE (v4.0 — unified SWFL protocol)
notes: ⚠ PRODIAMINE TIMING: Apply Visit 1 by January 15. Soil temps can hit 55°F germination window by late Jan in Sarasota/Man
⚠ CELSIUS CAP: Maximum 3 applications per property per year. Log every Celsius app. If maxed, use Blindside WDG or Three
⚠ K-FLOW ROTATION: Jun + Sep = K-Flow. Jul = Ca supplement. Aug = Mg/Ca supplement. Do NOT apply K-Flow every blackout m
★ NEW v4: N RATE CONTROL — Explicit lb N/1,000 per visit in Notes. Annual target: Basic/Std 1.5 lb N, Enh/Prem 3.0 lb N.
★ NEW v4: Soil-test branching — P index >80 skip 24-2-11, K >80 ppm skip K-Flow. Chinch IPM ≥20/sq ft threshold + MOA ro
★ NEW v4: FRAC rotation — Headway (11+3) Jan → Medallion (7) Feb → Torque (12) Oct. SpeedZone Apr weather-gated at 90°F.
★ NEW v4: Aug drive-by scout + Dec wellness touchpoint for Basic/Std. Thatch measurement V3+V10. Irrigation VWC audit V2
★ ST. AUGUSTINE IS ONE TRACK: Do not split the protocol into full-sun vs shade programs. Use site observations only as condition checks for stress, disease, irrigation, and herbicide safety.

site_condition_rules.stress_check: If turf is heat, drought, scalp, or disease stressed, hold PGR and hot herbicide until manager review.
site_condition_rules.herbicide_heat_gate: SpeedZone Southern is weather-gated at 90°F; use Celsius WG fallback when heat risk is high.
site_condition_rules.disease_check: Pull-test and inspect roots when thinning or patch symptoms are present; choose fungicide by diagnosis, not by sun/shade label.
site_condition_rules.irrigation_vwc_threshold: Use VWC/audit notes to adjust irrigation recommendations; do not create a separate St. Augustine shade program.

bermuda.name: ⚠ THIS IS BERMUDA ONLY — DO NOT USE THIS TRACK FOR ZOYSIA. S
notes: ⚠ SpeedZone Southern: NEVER apply above 90°F. In SWFL summer, use Celsius WG for Jul/Aug/Sep broadleaf instead.
⚠ NO Atrazine on Bermuda — Atrazine is ST. AUGUSTINE ONLY.
⚠ PGR: Primo Maxx every 3-4 weeks Mar-Oct. Document growth response each visit. Bermuda grows aggressively.
⚠ N budget: 3.0-4.0 lb N/K/yr. Explicit rates per visit below. Bermuda = high-input track.
★ NEW v4: SDS preventive fungicide calendar — Armada/Velista (FRAC 7) in Oct+Nov BEFORE soil temps <70°F. T-Storm replac
★ NEW v4: Armyworm IPM — soap flush threshold ≥3/sq ft. Curative rotation: Bifen (3A) → chlorantraniliprole (28) → spino
★ NEW v4: Mole cricket curative defined — Dylox 420 SL for adult activity beyond Acelepryn residual.
★ NEW v4: SpeedZone April weather-gated at 90°F. Soil-test branching. Thatch measurement V3+V10. Irrigation VWC audit.
★ NEW v4: PGR response documentation at each PGR visit — calibrate rate per property. Aug/Dec all-tier touchpoints.
★ NEW v4: FRAC rotation fixed — Armada (7) Oct → Armada (7) Nov → Torque (12) spring SDS followup. No FRAC 11 stacking.

zoysia.name: ⚠ THIS IS ZOYSIA ONLY — DO NOT USE THIS TRACK FOR BERMUDA. S
notes: ⚠ N budget: 2.0-3.0 lb N/K/yr MAX (UF/IFAS). Excess N = explosive thatch → severe Large Patch in fall.
⚠ PGR: Primo Maxx at CONSERVATIVE rates only. Document response. Over-regulation = months of thin turf.
⚠ Large Patch (Rhizoctonia): Zoysia is HIGHLY susceptible. Preventive fungicide Oct-Mar with FRAC rotation.
⚠ SpeedZone Southern: NEVER apply above 90°F. Use Celsius WG for Jul/Aug/Sep broadleaf.
⚠ NO Atrazine on Zoysia. NO Anuew EZ on Zoysia.
★ NEW v4: Large Patch FRAC rotation calendar — Headway (11+3) Oct → Medallion (7) Nov → Torque (12) Dec/Jan. No more tri
★ NEW v4: Large patch diagnostic criteria for techs — orange-brown rings with dark "smoke ring" advancing border.
★ NEW v4: Thatch measurement V3+V10. Zoysia trigger: >0.5" (lower than Bermuda 0.75" — denser thatch per inch).
★ NEW v4: SpeedZone April weather-gated at 90°F. Soil-test branching. PGR response documentation.
★ NEW v4: Irrigation VWC audit V2+V6. Aug all-tier scout. Dec all-tier touchpoint. Conditional ceiling $60.

bahia.name: TRACK D: BAHIA (5-10% of accounts) [v4.0 REVISED]
notes: ⚠ Bahia is LOW-INPUT turf. 2.0 lb N/K/yr MAX. More N = more mowing, NOT better quality.
⚠ Mole cricket is THE primary insect threat — not chinch bugs. Soapy flush scout, not leaf inspection.
⚠ NO PGR. NO Atrazine. Customer expectations: Bahia will NEVER look like irrigated St. Augustine.
⚠ K-FLOW ROTATION: Alternate with Ca/Mg supplements during blackout to prevent cation imbalance.
★ NEW v4: Irrigated vs. Non-irrigated classification at V1 — drives product load. Non-irrigated: skip K-Flow, skip Moist
★ NEW v4: Mole cricket IPM — soapy water flush method defined. Threshold ≥2 per 2 sq ft. Acelepryn preventive + Dylox cu
★ NEW v4: T-Storm removed from Oct default — fungicide now conditional on active dollar spot only. Low-input = no "just
★ NEW v4: Fire ant protocol — Topchoice broadcast ONCE/yr at V4 for documented history. 12-month control. Prevents callb
★ NEW v4: Crabgrass breakthrough curative defined — Drive XLR8 (quinclorac), labeled for Bahia.
★ NEW v4: SpeedZone Sep weather-gated at 90°F. Soil-test branching. Explicit N rates (0.75 lb N/1K per app).
★ NEW v4: Seed head customer communication at V1+V7. Aug all-tier scout. Dec all-tier touchpoint. $60 conditional ceilin
★ NEW v4: Customer expectation framework structured at V1, V7 (seed heads), V12 (dormancy). Reduces cancellations.

★ SpeedZone Southern WEATHER-GATED ($5.95)
K-Flow 0-0-25 ($2.18)

visits.secondary:

— provides 12-month control, prevents callbacks
★ IF forecast >90°F → Celsius WG instead of SpeedZone
Mole cricket: Acelepryn covers nymphs
visits.notes: Acelepryn covers mole cricket nymphs — critical. ★ Fire ant: Topchoice broadcast for documented history (not "if severe"). ★ SpeedZone weather-gated. ★ K-Flow skipped on non-irrigated.

Chelated Iron Plus ($1.52)
Chelated AM + Micros ($1.40)

★ NON-IRRIGATED: Fe+micros only, skip K-Flow
visits.secondary:
Dismiss if sedge ($4.36)
★ Crabgrass check — if pre-em breaking down:
Drive XLR8 (quinclorac) spot treatment ($5.50)
Labeled for Bahia, effective on crabgrass
visits.notes: No fert — only 2 N apps/yr. Micros + K (irrigated only). ★ Crabgrass curative DEFINED: Drive XLR8 (quinclorac). ★ K-Flow conditional on irrigation status.

visits.primary:

Chelated Iron Plus ($1.52)
High Mn Combo ($1.33)
visits.secondary:
★ MOLE CRICKET SOAP FLUSH #2:
Same method as Feb (2 tbsp/gal, 2 sq ft)
Damage shows as spongy/lifted turf
If ≥2 per 2 sq ft: Dylox 420 SL curative ($12.53)
visits.notes: BLACKOUT. K-Flow (irrigated only, soil-test gated). ★ Mole cricket soap flush #2 with defined threshold and Dylox curative. ★ Non-irrigated properties: reduced product load.

visits.primary:

Green Flo 6-0-0 Ca ($1.39)
visits.secondary:

★ CUSTOMER EXPECTATION TALK #2 — SEED HEADS:
"Bahia produces seed heads Jun-Aug. This is normal,
healthy biology — not a problem. Mowing frequency
controls the l
visits.notes: Celsius app 2. Ca replaces K-Flow. ★ Seed head customer talk STRUCTURED — proactive, not reactive. Mowing upsell opportunity if mowing division/partner available.

visits.primary:

Premium: Chelated AM + Micros ($1.40)
Premium: High Mn Combo ($1.33)
★ NON-IRRIGATED: skip Moisture Manager
visits.secondary:

★ MOLE CRICKET SOAP FLUSH #3 — peak damage
★ If active: Dylox 420 SL ($12.53)
★ Conditional ceiling >$60 YTD = reprice flag

visits.primary:
BLACKOUT — K-Flow 0-0-25 ($2.18)
★ NON-IRRIGATED: skip K-Flow
★ SpeedZone Southern WEATHER-GATED ($5.95)
★ IF >90°F → Celsius WG instead
visits.secondary:
Dismiss for sedge ($4.36)
★ Crabgrass breakthrough: Drive XLR8 spot ($5.50)
★ Early Sep SWFL can still hit 90°F+
visits.notes: K-Flow returns (irrigated only). ★ SpeedZone NOW WEATHER-GATED same as all other tracks. ★ Crabgrass curative defined: Drive XLR8. Blackout ends Sep 30.

visits.primary:
LESCO 24-0-11 PolyPlus fert ($8.68)
★ T-Storm CONDITIONAL — ONLY if dollar spot active ($6.15)
Chelated Iron Plus ($1.52)
visits.secondary:

★ Topchoice fall app ONLY if spring Topchoice not applied ($8.22)
Soil re-test problem props

visits.primary:
SpeedZone Southern + NIS broadleaf ($5.95)
LESCO 0-0-18 Bio KMAG ($7.21)
visits.secondary:
NO Atrazine on Bahia — SpeedZone only
★ Irrigation winterize (if irrigated)
★ Non-irrigated: brief visual check only
visits.notes: SpeedZone replaces Atrazine. K for winter. ★ Non-irrigated properties: abbreviated visit — visual + K only.

visits.primary:

Premium: LESCO 0-0-18 Bio KMAG ($7.21)
Premium: Chelated Iron Plus ($1.52)
visits.secondary:

★ Annual report ALL tiers (simplified for Basic)
★ CUSTOMER EXPECTATION TALK #3 — DORMANCY:
"Bahia goes dormant earlier and longer than St. Aug.
Brown
visits.notes: ★ MAJOR: Basic/Std Dec touchpoint. ★ Dormancy expectation talk STRUCTURED. Bahia-specific door hanger. Annual report. Prevents winter/spring cancellations.

tree_shrub.name: 10/10 SWFL Tree & Shrub Protocol
notes: Track rotation by IRAC/FRAC group and active ingredient. Merit, Zylam, and IMA-jet all count as Group 4A/neonic-style pressure.
Snapshot 2.5TG remains the quarterly visual differentiator: visits 1, 4, 7, and 10. Apply to beds only and water in with irrigation or rainfall.

CRM ordinance zone is required. Sarasota/Venice and Manatee/Parrish landscape N/P blackout runs June 1-Sept. 30. North Port turf blackout starts April 1, but landscape plants are route-gated separately. Unknown zone defaults to Sarasota/Manatee conservative.
Palm/tree injections are add-ons only. Minimum $35/palm, not included in Tree & Shrub tiers.
Do not make oil + copper, oil + broad-spectrum pyrethroid, or neonic systemics a default production tank. Use plant safety, pollinator status, pest ID, and life stage gates.
safety_rules: CRM ordinance zone required before fertilizer
Oil applications require plant/weather safety check
Copper is separate from routine oil tanks
IRAC/FRAC log required after every product use
Injections are separate add-ons, not tier inclusions

service_area_rules.rule: No nitrogen or phosphorus landscape fertilizer June 1-Sept. 30.
service_area_rules.sop_impact: Hold 8-2-12, 13-0-13, alfalfa meal, and other N/P bed products during blackout.

service_area_rules.rule: North Port's April 1-Sept. 30 stricter rule is turf-focused; landscape plant fertility must be route-gated and documented separately.
service_area_rules.sop_impact: Do not confuse turf blackout with landscape beds. If uncertain, use Sarasota/Manatee conservative logic.

service_area_rules.rule: No nitrogen or phosphorus landscape fertilizer June 1-Sept. 30.
service_area_rules.sop_impact: Same summer landscape N/P hold as Sarasota/Manatee routes.

service_area_rules.rule: Default to Sarasota/Manatee conservative rule until verified.
service_area_rules.sop_impact: Block summer N/P fertilizer until the property zone is corrected.

visit_types: Granular bed visit: spreader only for Snapshot and legal palm/ornamental fertilizer.
Foliar PHC visit: spray rig or FlowZone only for oil, IGR, micros, fungicide, insecticide.
Drench/systemic visit: drench gun, soil injection, or targeted root-zone soak.
Injection add-on: Arborjet only, billed separately.
Scout-only visit: photos, pest ID, palm health notes, and customer report.
tree_shrub.production_rule: Do not run a spreader and then fire up the Honda rig on the same standard Tree & Shrub visit unless it is a premium or corrective job.

calibration.tank_110_gal_formula: Amount for 110 gal = label rate per 100 gal x 1.10
calibration.flowzone_2_5_gal_formula: Amount for 2.5 gal = label rate per 100 gal x 0.025
snapshot_rates: Clean maintained beds: 2.3 lb/1,000 sq ft
Moderate weed pressure: 3.45 lb/1,000 sq ft
Heavy pressure: 4.6 lb/1,000 sq ft only where label/site permits
calibration.palm_fertilizer_rate: 8-2-12 palm fertilizer at 1.5 lb per 100 sq ft canopy/root-zone area where ordinance allows.
calibration.ornamental_fertilizer_rate: 13-0-13 at 0.25-0.50 lb N/1,000 sq ft equivalent unless site, plant type, and timing justify otherwise.

mixing_order: Add dry flowables / WDG / DF first.
Keep agitation running and apply same day.

insect_miticide: 4A/neonic-style pressure: Merit, Zylam, IMA-jet. Selective use only; track annually.
28: Mainspring for caterpillars, whiteflies, leafminers, and resistance management.
7C: Distance IGR for eggs, nymphs, and crawlers.
16: Talus IGR for immature whitefly and scale stages.
Oil/contact: SuffOil-X for crawlers, mites, and soft-bodied pests when plant/weather safe.
Spinosyn: Conserve for caterpillar/thrips-type work where labeled.
Miticide: Floramite for confirmed mites only.
Broad-spectrum rescue: Talstar or Sevin only when documented pest/site conditions justify it.
fungicide_disease: P07: KPHITE 7LP for root/oomycete/Phytophthora-type support where justified.
M01/M1: Copper as a contact protectant for labeled leaf/bacterial disease.
11+3: Headway only for labeled ornamental disease where justified.
11: Artavia is not a fresh rotation after Headway's FRAC 11 component.
3: Propizol only for labeled diagnosed tree diseases; not routine palm health.

products: Palm-Jet Mg for diagnosed magnesium deficiency.
Mn-Jet Fe for diagnosed manganese/iron deficiency pattern.
IMA-jet for high-value tree/palm pest systemic where label/site fits; log as 4A/neonic pressure.
Propizol for diagnosed labeled tree disease, not routine palm health.

crm_required_fields: Photos for decline, outbreaks, and injection candidates

tiers.four_x: Jan, Apr/May, Jul/Aug, Oct/Nov. Best for cleaner properties with low pest history.
tiers.six_x: Jan, Mar, May, Jul, Sep, Oct/Nov. Best for SWFL estates, palms, ficus, ixora, crotons, and whitefly/scale-prone landscapes.

tree_shrub.final_operating_sentence: Every tree/shrub visit must be legal by ordinance zone, safe for pollinators and plant stress, justified by pest life stage or seasonal nutrition, completed in one production mode when possible, and documented by product, rate, IRAC/FRAC group, target pest, plant group, and customer-facing note.

visits.visit_type: Granular bed visit + targeted drench if justified
visits.main_goal: Clean visual start, winter palm nutrition, bed pre-emergent, and documented systemic only where needed.
visits.primary:
Snapshot 2.5TG Q1: 2.3-3.45 lb/1,000 sq ft beds; water in
8-2-12 palm fertilizer: 1.5 lb/100 sq ft canopy/root-zone where ordinance allows
13-0-13 ornamental fertilizer: 0.25-0.50 lb N/1,000 sq ft equivalent where needed
visits.secondary:
Merit 2F drench only for documented scale/whitefly/borer history; log as IRAC 4A
SuffOil-X 1.0-1.5% if scale crawlers are active and plant/weather safe
Organic acidifier only for high-pH/chlorosis properties
visits.notes: Spreader-first visit. Do not apply neonicotinoids to blooming plants or where bees are actively foraging. New accounts require plant inventory, bed sq ft, palm canopy sq ft, and photos.

visits.main_goal: Clean up scale/mites/whitefly pressure, correct micros, and avoid unnecessary systemics.
visits.primary:
SuffOil-X: 1.0% standard, 1.5% only with active scale/mites and safe weather
Iron Plus: label rate
Mn Combo: label rate
CleanKelp: label rate as foliar or drench per product
visits.secondary:
Conserve SC 0.1-0.2 fl oz/gal for caterpillars where labeled
EDDHA iron for high-pH chlorosis
Floramite 4-8 fl oz/100 gal for confirmed mites
Labeled ornamental fungicide only when disease/history warrants
visits.notes: Do not use turf disease language here. For T&S, document leaf spot, anthracnose, Phytophthora/root rot, palm disease diagnosis, mites, scale, or whitefly by plant group.

visits.main_goal: Break whitefly/scale cycles before summer without back-to-back 4A pressure.
visits.primary:
Kontos: 1.7-3.4 fl oz/100 gal, IRAC 23 non-neonic systemic
Mainspring GNL: 4-8 fl oz/100 gal, IRAC 28 where target pest fits
Distance IGR: 6-8 fl oz/100 gal for whitefly/scale nymphs or crawlers
KPHITE 7LP: 1-2 qt/100 gal foliar, FRAC P07 where root/oomycete risk is justified
visits.secondary:
Zylam rescue only for severe documented pressure; log as 4A/neonic pressure and apply pollinator gate
NutriRoot and Cytogro are premium/stressed-account support, not universal
Inspect ficus and spiraling whitefly undersides, honeydew, and sooty mold
visits.notes: March is intentionally the non-neonic systemic/IGR month unless severe pressure justifies rescue chemistry.

visits.visit_type: Granular bed visit + optional targeted foliar
visits.main_goal: Visible bed cleanup, Q2 nutrition, spring crawler check, and route-speed discipline.
visits.primary:
Snapshot 2.5TG Q2: 2.3-3.45 lb/1,000 sq ft beds; water in
8-2-12 palm fertilizer: 1.5 lb/100 sq ft canopy/root-zone where ordinance allows
13-0-13 ornamental fertilizer: 0.25-0.50 lb N/1,000 sq ft equivalent where needed
visits.secondary:
Shortstop PGR add-on only on healthy established hedges
SuffOil-X 1.0-1.5% only if scale crawlers are active and safe
Distance or Talus for whitefly nymphs/crawlers
Conserve or Mainspring for caterpillars where labeled
Talstar only as documented rescue, not default
visits.notes: Do not make oil plus pyrethroid a default combo. Broad-spectrum insecticide requires documented pest pressure and mite/beneficial risk review.

visits.main_goal: Finish legal nutrition before June blackout in Sarasota/Manatee and use non-neonic systemic rotation.
visits.primary:
Kontos: 1.7-3.4 fl oz/100 gal, IRAC 23
Mainspring GNL: 4-8 fl oz/100 gal if target pest/resistance history fits
Iron Plus + Mn Combo: label rate
8-2-12 palm fertilizer and 13-0-13 ornamental fertilizer only if needed and legal before blackout
visits.secondary:
Floramite for confirmed mites
EDDHA iron for high-pH chlorosis
Palm weevil inspection and diagnostic/injection quote if crown, trunk wound, or decline symptoms support it
SuperThrive/root drench only for premium/stressed accounts
visits.notes: Last clean nutrition window before June 1 for Sarasota/Manatee landscape accounts. North Port landscape fertility must be route-tagged and not confused with turf.

visits.main_goal: Begin Sarasota/Manatee N/P blackout, manage summer pest/disease pressure, and avoid heat injury.
visits.primary:
SuffOil-X: 1.0% early morning only when plant/weather safe
KPHITE 7LP: 1-2 qt/100 gal, FRAC P07 where root/oomycete risk is justified
Liquid copper: 2-4 tsp/gal for labeled leaf spot/bacterial disease, separate from oil
Fe/Mn micros: label rate where deficiency symptoms justify
visits.secondary:
Conserve or Mainspring for caterpillars
Distance or Talus for whitefly/scale nymphs
Floramite for confirmed mites
Scout/photo/customer note when heat stress makes chemistry the wrong move
visits.notes: No N/P landscape fertilizer in Sarasota/Manatee June 1-Sept. 30. Do not run Merit as a standard June drench.

visits.main_goal: Keep beds clean during summer while maintaining a zero-N/P route for Sarasota/Manatee.
visits.primary:
Snapshot 2.5TG Q3: 2.3-3.45 lb/1,000 sq ft beds; water in
Scout bed weeds, whitefly, scale crawlers, palm deficiency symptoms, and heat stress
Sarasota/Manatee: zero N/P landscape fertilizer during blackout
visits.secondary:
Mainspring GNL 4-8 fl oz/100 gal for whitefly/caterpillar pressure
Distance or Talus for scale crawlers/nymphs
SuffOil-X 1.0% only if plant/weather safe
AzaMax organic option where label/site/pest fit
True 0-N/0-P corrective product only if legal and diagnosis supports it
visits.notes: July palm/ornamental N/P fertilizer is blocked on Sarasota/Manatee landscape accounts. Snapshot is fine; 8-2-12 and 13-0-13 are not.

visits.main_goal: Prevent summer collapse with low burn-risk pest correction and high observation.
visits.primary:
Mainspring GNL: 4-8 fl oz/100 gal, IRAC 28 where target pest fits
Distance IGR: 6-8 fl oz/100 gal for whitefly/scale nymphs
SuffOil-X: 1.0% only if safe
Cytogro and CleanKelp for premium/stressed accounts
visits.secondary:
Floramite for confirmed mites
No alfalfa meal or other N/P bed products during Sarasota/Manatee blackout
Severe whitefly: pair adult/nymph strategy to life stage, not calendar habit
Heat stress: document and communicate instead of adding chemistry
visits.notes: August is low product, high observation. Customer value comes from catching decline early and explaining plant stress clearly.

visits.main_goal: Break late-summer scale/whitefly life cycles and prepare fall nutrition without N/P until October in Sarasota/Manatee.
visits.primary:
Talus IGR: label rate for whitefly/scale nymphs, IRAC 16
Distance IGR: 6-8 fl oz/100 gal if rotation calls for IRAC 7C
Iron Plus + Mn Combo: label rate
SuffOil-X: 1.0% only if crawlers are present and safe
visits.secondary:
Headway only for labeled ornamental disease and only if FRAC history allows
Shortstop PGR add-on only on healthy established hedges
4A/neonic systemic only if documented and annual 4A log allows
Diagnose palm K/Mg/Mn/Fe before treating
visits.notes: Final blackout month for Sarasota/Manatee. Do not make Merit automatic with Talus; use life-stage IGR strategy unless systemic need is documented.

visits.visit_type: Granular bed visit + optional disease/root PHC
visits.main_goal: Restart legal nutrition after blackout, reset beds, and protect fall disease/root-prone ornamentals.
visits.primary:
Snapshot 2.5TG Q4: 2.3-3.45 lb/1,000 sq ft beds; water in
8-2-12 palm fertilizer: 1.5 lb/100 sq ft canopy/root-zone where appropriate
13-0-13 ornamental fertilizer: 0.25-0.50 lb N/1,000 sq ft equivalent where needed
KPHITE 7LP: 1-2 qt/100 gal foliar or labeled drench, FRAC P07 where justified
visits.secondary:
Kontos 1.7-3.4 fl oz/100 gal if target pest fits
Distance or Talus for whitefly nymphs/crawlers
Fall scale flush inspection
High-value palms: offer injection assessment, not automatic injection
visits.notes: Visible fall reset: Snapshot + legal fertility + fall inspection. Keep this as a granular-first production visit whenever possible.

visits.main_goal: Cool-season cleanup, micronutrient correction, root support, and diagnosed injection add-on opportunities.
visits.primary:
SuffOil-X: 1.0-1.5% if plant/weather safe
NutriRoot: label rate where site supports root nutrition
Mn Combo: label rate
Organic acidifier only where pH/chlorosis data supports it
EDDHA iron for high-pH chlorosis sites
visits.secondary:
Mn-Jet Fe add-on for diagnosed palm Mn/Fe deficiency pattern
Palm-Jet Mg add-on for diagnosed Mg deficiency
Propizol only for labeled diagnosed tree disease
Artavia or Headway only if label fits and FRAC log allows
visits.notes: Call this cool-season cleanup, not dormant spray. SWFL ornamentals are not truly dormant like northern landscapes.

visits.main_goal: Retention, customer proof of value, winter palm nutrition where due, and next-year correction plan.
visits.primary:
8-2-12 palm fertilizer: 1.5 lb/100 sq ft canopy/root-zone if due and appropriate
Cytogro: label rate for premium/stressed accounts
EDDHA iron: label rate for high-pH chlorosis
Annual health report with photos, IRAC/FRAC history, Snapshot history, fertility dates, pest pressure, and next-year plan
visits.secondary:
Zylam only if pest history/pressure and 4A log allow
Do not treat blooming plants with bee-toxic systemic/contact products while bees forage
Quote diagnostic/injection add-on for palm decline
Note soil pH, irrigation water, or salt issues for chronic chlorosis
visits.notes: Retention month. The annual report should read like an agronomic plant-health-care program, not random spraying.

pest.name: Residential Pest Control Protocol Templates
notes: Use these templates as service-line SOPs. Product quantities still come from Inventory service-product usage so estimate pricing stays rule-based while margin checks stay inventory-aware.
Rotate active ingredient classes when a callback shows live activity after the expected control window.
Do not use repellent sprays directly on active ant or German roach trails when bait transfer is the objective.

visits.primary:
Exterior general pest perimeter
Exterior perimeter band
Applied repellent solution with surfactant to perimeter
Applied non-repellent solution to perimeter (6-ft band + crack & crevice)
Interior crack-and-crevice treatment only if activity is present
Sweep eaves, windows, doors, and lanai frames
visits.secondary:
WDG or granular bait in landscape beds
Glue boards in garage or utility areas
Escalate moisture, exclusion, or sanitation issues in notes
visits.notes: Default recurring pest template for quarterly, bimonthly, and monthly visits. Keep interior treatment targeted.

visits.primary:
German roach cleanout
Cockroach gel bait in kitchen, bath, hinges, appliances, and plumbing voids
Crack-and-crevice treatment
Insect growth regulator point-source or aerosol where label allows
visits.secondary:
Vacuum visible aggregation before application
Place monitors and document count/location
Schedule 10-14 day follow-up when live activity is confirmed
visits.notes: Non-repellent and bait-first protocol. Do not contaminate bait placements with pyrethroid spray.

visits.primary:
Ant service
Identify species and trail source before applying
Ant gel or granular bait along active trails
Non-repellent treatment (interior + exterior) for ghost or trailing ants
visits.secondary:
Treat conducive entry points and moisture sources
Avoid repellent spray on active trails
Recheck bait acceptance at callback
visits.notes: Species ID drives product choice. Ghost ants require patience and non-repellent transfer.

visits.primary:
Flea/Tick Treatment
Interior crack/edge treatment where pets rest
Exterior shaded harborage, fence lines, and transition zones
IGR where label allows
visits.secondary:
Customer must vacuum before and after service
Pet treatment is required through veterinarian or approved pet product
Second treatment may be needed in 10-14 days
visits.notes: Do not guarantee resolution unless pet treatment and vacuum protocol are followed.

visits.primary:
Rodent monitoring / bait
Inspect attic, garage, exterior penetrations, roof returns, AC chase, and utility entries
Rodenticide blocks in tamper-resistant stations where label allows
Snap traps in attic/garage when activity is active
visits.secondary:
Document exclusion findings with photos
Escalate seal-up proposal when entry points are active
Remove carcasses and reset traps on follow-up
visits.notes: Baiting without exclusion is monitoring, not remediation. Separate exclusion scope when needed.

visits.primary:
Mosquito Barrier
Essential Barrier: bifenthrin adulticide directed to foliage, fence lines, shaded resting zones, lanai perimeter, and pool cage edges
Precision Barrier: gamma-cyhalothrin adulticide for heavy vegetation, water adjacency, pool cages, and higher residual expectations
Pyriproxyfen + novaluron IGR support where breeding pressure exists
visits.secondary:
Inspect drains, planters, saucers, bromeliads, gutters, tarps, toys, and other water-holding sites
Dump standing water where practical and document sources that cannot be removed
Use mosquito stations or Bti dunk tablets when breeding sources cannot be fully eliminated
Avoid blooming plants and pollinator activity windows
visits.notes: Use gas-powered backpack sprayer for directed barrier application. Peak pressure May-Oct. Wind, rain, inaccessible water, and source-reduction notes must be documented.

termite.name: Termite Service Protocol Templates
notes: Termite protocols are compliance-sensitive. These templates define workflow and product family, while final application must follow the product label, graph/diagram, and state documentation requirements.
Keep bait, liquid, foam, and wood-treatment scopes separate because cost drivers, warranty language, and follow-up cadence differ.
Use Inventory service-product usage for COGS visibility; do not make live estimate prices depend directly on inventory quantity.

visits.primary:
Termite Inspection / WDO Support
Inspect foundation, plumbing penetrations, garage, attic access, windows, bath traps, expansion joints, and moisture areas
Document swarmers, wings, mud tubes, damaged wood, conducive conditions, and prior treatment evidence
visits.secondary:
Recommend bait, liquid, foam, or Bora-Care scope based on finding location
Collect photos before treatment proposal
Escalate active structural findings immediately
visits.notes: Inspection is the qualifying step. Treatment scope must match evidence and construction type.

visits.primary:
Termite Bait Installation / Monitoring
Install Trelona, Advance, or HexPro stations per label and site plan
Place stations around accessible perimeter and high-risk conducive zones
Record station count, spacing exceptions, obstructions, and diagram notes
visits.secondary:
Inspect stations on recurring cadence
Replace consumed bait or wood monitors as needed
Escalate active hits to treatment recommendation
visits.notes: COGS should scale with station count. Inventory service usage tracks per-station product cost.

visits.primary:
Liquid Perimeter / Trench and Rod
Termidor SC or approved termiticide per label
Treat soil at foundation, expansion joints, plumbing penetrations, and active tube zones
Diagram treated linear footage and gallons applied
visits.secondary:
Drill slab or paver zones only when required and approved
Protect wells, drains, water bodies, and inaccessible areas per label
Schedule reinspection based on activity
visits.notes: High-liability workflow. Requires exact label rate, volume, diagram, and access notes.

visits.primary:
Foam Drill / Localized Void Treatment
Termidor Foam or approved foam product into active galleries, wall voids, or localized inaccessible areas
Confirm target void, drill pattern, and customer approval before application
visits.secondary:
Patch/clean drill points where in scope
Pair with bait or liquid when structural risk is broader than localized activity
Photo before and after
visits.notes: Localized treatment only. Do not represent as whole-structure protection unless paired with a full program.

visits.primary:
Bora-Care / Wood Treatment
Apply Bora-Care or approved borate to accessible raw wood, attic members, crawlspace framing, or new construction wood
Measure treatable surface area and document inaccessible/painted/sealed areas
visits.secondary:
Protect finished surfaces and insulation
Use foam or spray method as label and access require
Recommend follow-up inspection for active evidence
visits.notes: Best for accessible wood. Cost scales by treatable sqft and dilution/application method.

visits.primary:
Annual Renewal / Warranty Inspection
Inspect stations or treated zones, conducive conditions, moisture, wood-to-ground contact, and customer-reported activity
Update diagram and photos
Confirm renewal eligibility and warranty status
visits.secondary:
Quote corrective work for conducive conditions outside warranty scope
Replace bait/monitors as needed
Schedule next inspection cadence
visits.notes: Renewal workflow protects liability and keeps the graph/current condition record clean.
